description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a bright orange and red color scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other and connected by a series of struts and wires. The fuselage is relatively short and rounded, with an open cockpit situated near the front, just behind the upper wing. The tail section includes a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both in matching orange hues. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The engine is mounted at the nose, with a visible propeller and exhaust pipes extending beneath the lower wing. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, emphasizing lightweight construction and external bracing.
description: A classic orange and red biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, evoking early aviation designs.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and the tail assembly.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, struts, and the nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_2: Top-down view from the opposite side, revealing the wing structure and tail surfaces.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail fin, horizontal stabilizer, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
